metadata.description: EtherFi is a liquid staking protocol that issues eETH (and its wrapped variant weETH) against ETH deposits. Staked ETH is restaked into EigenLayer strategies and Lido stETH positions, earning both consensus-layer and restaking rewards. The protocol is fully upgradeable: most core contracts sit behind EIP-1967 proxies controlled by a 3-day timelock, with a secondary 8-hour timelock governing operational parameters. A 3/5 multisig and a 4/7 multisig share proposer/executor roles on these timelocks, and several EOAs hold pauser or node-operator privileges. External dependencies include EigenLayer (delegation, pod management, strategy deposits), Lido (stETH minting and withdrawals), Curve (liquidity pools), Coinbase cbETH, and LayerZero (cross-chain messaging). Total capital at risk across admins is $1.58B.

admins.name: EtherFi Governance 4/7 Multisig
admins.description: A 4-of-7 multisig that holds proposer and executor roles on the main 3-day EtherFiTimelock. Can schedule, execute, and cancel timelock transactions. Does not control any contracts directly — all actions are gated by the timelock delay.

admins.name: EtherFi Main Timelock (3d)
admins.description: Primary governance timelock with a 3-day delay. Controls upgrades to the majority of protocol contracts (LiquidityPool, EtherFiNodesManager, StakingManager, TNFT, WithdrawRequestNFT, Liquifier, MembershipManager, and others), manages EtherFiRestaker EigenLayer operations (delegation, strategy deposits, withdrawals), and governs protocol parameters across AuctionManager, EtherFiOracle, ProtocolRevenueManager, and MembershipNFT. Holds proposer/executor roles shared with the 4/7 multisig. Controls $1.58B in direct capital exposure across 155 permissioned functions.

mitigations.description: Fee amounts must be multiples of 0.001 ether and <= 65.535 ether (uint16.max * 0.001 ether)

admins.name: Zero Address (Renounced)
admins.description: Represents renounced permissions. Holds minting rights on DummyTokenUpgradeable, acceptOwnership on RoleRegistry, and rescueERC20 on external Coinbase cbETH and Curve WrapTokenV3ETH. These permissions are effectively burned.

admins.name: EtherFi Operations 3/5 Multisig
admins.description: A 3-of-5 multisig with direct control over operational functions: pausing/unpausing LiquidityPool, EtherFiAdmin, EtherFiRateLimiter, and EtherFiRedemptionManager; managing DummyTokenUpgradeable roles; triggering batch validator creation; and configuring rate limiter consumers. Can also recover stuck ETH/ERC20/ERC721 from eETH and weETH token contracts. Controls $46.94M in direct capital across 35 functions.

admins.name: EtherFiAdmin Contract
admins.description: An upgradeable admin contract that aggregates multiple protocol operations behind a single caller. Manages validator lifecycle (approval, confirmation, funding), LiquidityPool parameters (validator size, fee recipient, restaking config), pause/unpause, and protocol fee payments. Owned by the main 3-day timelock. Controls $46.94M in direct capital.

mitigations.description: Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
Validator size parameter bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH

admins.name: Pauser EOA (0x9AF1...844D)
admins.description: An EOA with pause-only privileges. Can pause EtherFiAdmin, LiquidityPool, EtherFiRateLimiter, EtherFiNodesManager, and EtherFiRedemptionManager. Cannot unpause or modify any protocol state beyond halting operations.

admins.name: EtherFi Operational Timelock (8h)
admins.description: Secondary timelock with an 8-hour delay, governing operational parameters. Controls EtherFiAdmin settings (validator task batch size, rebase APR bounds), EtherFiNodesManager node operations (sweepFunds, EigenPod management, forwarded calls), EtherFiRedemptionManager parameters (capacity, refill rate, exit fees), and LiquidityPool validator configuration (validator size, spawner registration, fee recipient). Controls $46.94M in direct capital.

mitigations.description: Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH

admins.name: Node Operator EOA (0x1258...1B0F)
admins.description: An EOA with node operation privileges. Can execute validator tasks and approval tasks on EtherFiAdmin, batch-create beacon validators on LiquidityPool, and manage EigenLayer operations on EtherFiNodesManager (create pods, set proof submitters, queue/complete ETH and strategy withdrawals, link legacy validators). Operational role with no direct upgrade or parameter-setting authority.

mitigations.description: Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH

admins.name: RoleRegistry Contract
admins.description: An upgradeable role-based access control contract that gates operations on WithdrawRequestNFT (finalize/invalidate/validate requests, pause, handle remainders) and Wrapped eETH Token (recover assets, upgrade proxy). The RoleRegistry itself is owned by the main 3-day timelock. Controls $6.24B in reachable capital through withdrawal and weETH management.

admins.name: Checkpoint EOA (0x7835...5d2A)
admins.description: An EOA with limited EtherFiNodesManager privileges: can start and verify checkpoint proofs, and forward external/EigenPod calls. No capital control or upgrade authority.

dependencies.name: Ethereum Beacon Chain DepositContract
dependencies.description: Ethereum's native Beacon Chain deposit contract. EtherFi deposits 32 ETH per validator through StakingManager to activate new beacon validators. An immutable system contract with no admin risk.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer EigenPodManager
dependencies.description: Manages EigenPod creation and beacon chain proof verification for EtherFi's restaked validators. Called by EtherFiNodesManager and EtherFiRestaker. Upgradeable by EigenLayer governance.

dependencies.name: LayerZero Unknown Contract
dependencies.description: A LayerZero infrastructure contract that interacts with EtherFi's cross-chain messaging components. Writes to protocol state via the OFT adapter layer.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer DelegationManager
dependencies.description: Handles operator delegation and withdrawal queuing for EtherFi's restaked positions. EtherFiRestaker delegates to EigenLayer operators and queues/completes strategy withdrawals through this contract. Upgradeable by EigenLayer governance.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer StrategyManager
dependencies.description: Manages EtherFi's restaking strategy deposits into EigenLayer. EtherFiRestaker deposits stETH into EigenLayer strategies via this contract. The restaker holds $1.54B in total positions across EigenLayer and Lido combined.

dependencies.name: Lido stETH Token
dependencies.description: Lido's liquid staked ETH token. The Liquifier contract interacts with stETH for liquidity operations, and EtherFiRestaker holds stETH positions deposited into EigenLayer strategies.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer RewardsCoordinator
dependencies.description: Distributes restaking rewards to EtherFi's restaker contract. EtherFiRestaker sets its rewards claimer through the DelegationManager which interacts with this coordinator.

dependencies.name: Lido WithdrawalQueueERC721
dependencies.description: Lido's withdrawal queue. EtherFiRestaker requests stETH-to-ETH withdrawals through this contract when rebalancing or exiting Lido positions.

dependencies.name: Coinbase cbETH Token
dependencies.description: Coinbase's wrapped staked ETH token. The Liquifier can accept and process cbETH deposits as an alternative liquid staking token input.

dependencies.name: Curve WrapTokenV3ETH
dependencies.description: Curve wrapper contract used by the Liquifier for token wrapping operations in liquidity management workflows.

dependencies.name: Curve Vyper_contract
dependencies.description: A Curve liquidity pool contract used for token swaps in the protocol's liquidity management operations.

dependencies.name: LayerZero EndpointV2
dependencies.description: LayerZero's cross-chain messaging endpoint. Used by EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH and EtherfiOFTAdapterUpgradeable for cross-chain eETH/weETH bridging and L1-L2 sync operations. Immutable contract with configurable message libraries.

funds.name: LiquidityPool (ETH)
funds.description: The main liquidity pool holding user-deposited ETH awaiting validator deployment or withdrawal processing. Holds $46.94M primarily in ETH, representing the protocol's liquid reserves available for staking and redemptions.

funds.name: Treasury (ETH)
funds.description: Protocol treasury holding accumulated fees and reserves. Contains $21.03K in ETH, controlled by the main 3-day timelock.

funds.name: eETH Token
funds.description: The ether.fi ETH rebasing token contract. eETH represents staked ETH positions with a current market capitalization of $6.62B.

funds.name: Wrapped eETH (weETH)
funds.description: The non-rebasing wrapped eETH token. Holds $6.24B in eETH and other tokens, representing wrapped staking positions used for DeFi integrations and cross-chain bridging.

funds.name: EtherFiRestaker (EigenLayer Positions)
funds.description: Holds EtherFi's restaked positions in EigenLayer and Lido. $1.54B in DeFi positions split between EigenLayer deposits/yield (stETH restaked) and Lido staked ETH. This is the largest single concentration of protocol capital.

mitigations.description: Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
Validator size parameter bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH
